pathways.label: Non-conviction fingerprint and criminal-history expungement under Idaho Code § 67-3004(10)
pathways.summary: A person arrested or served with a criminal summons who was not charged by indictment or information within one year may submit a written request to Idaho State Police. A person acquitted of all offenses arising from the incident, or whose all charges were dismissed, may request expungement and court-file sealing. This route does not apply to a dismissal granted under Idaho Code § 19-2604(1), including the withheld-judgment situation described by the source. The application requires charging documentation plus a certified acquittal or dismissal order; incomplete applications are returned. The source says a person convicted of a sex offense…
ruleClauses: A person arrested or served with a criminal summons who was not charged by indictment or information within one year may submit a written request to Idaho State Police.
A person acquitted of all offenses arising from the incident, or whose all charges were dismissed, may request expungement and court-file sealing.
This route does not apply to a dismissal granted under Idaho Code § 19-2604(1), including the withheld-judgment situation described by the source.
The application requires charging documentation plus a certified acquittal or dismissal order; incomplete applications are returned.
The source says a person convicted of a sex offense should not submit this form.
The Idaho State Police expungement application expressly says the non-conviction expungement process does not apply when a conviction was followed by a withheld judgment under § 19-2604(1).
The frontend must route this answer to state-specific review or a separate remedy profile, not the § 67-3004(10) application.
The exact criminal charges, including the statute(s) you were charged under and whether an offense was a misdemeanor or a felony.
If applicable, the date that you were no longer under the supervision of probation or parole.
The date your sentence and all conditions were completed, including any fines and restitution paid.
Obtain the case number from the court, or the iCourt Portal at: https://mycourts.idaho.gov/. If
you cannot locate your case information or have any questions about your court record, contact
the court where the case was filed.
What offenses may be eligible for shielding?
Only non-violent, non-assaultive offenses are included in the Clean Slate Act for shielding records
from public disclosure. You can review eligibility under the Clean Slate Act in Idaho Code section
If you are unsure whether you meet the conditions for shielding your record from public
disclosure, you should consult with an attorney. Please note, you can only have one Clean Slate
Act shielding petition granted in your lifetime. You may want to seek legal advice for help
determining the timing and offense eligibility before filing.

Fill out the Petition to Shield Records from Public Disclosure.

waitingRules: A person arrested or served with a criminal summons who was not charged by indictment or information within one year may submit a written request to Idaho State Police.
The source requires at least five years after completion of sentence, including probation, parole, fines, and restitution.
exclusionRules: This route does not apply to a dismissal granted under Idaho Code § 19-2604(1), including the withheld-judgment situation described by the source.
The Idaho State Police expungement application expressly says the non-conviction expungement process does not apply when a conviction was followed by a withheld judgment under § 19-2604(1).

pathways.label: Clean Slate shielding under Idaho Code § 67-3004(11)
pathways.summary: The source requires at least five years after completion of sentence, including probation, parole, fines, and restitution. Only nonviolent, non-assaultive offenses covered by Idaho Code § 67-3004(11)(a)-(c) may qualify. Only one offense, or offenses from a single incident or transaction, may be shielded, and only one Clean Slate petition may be granted in a lifetime. The petition is filed in the county court where the underlying case was filed, with no filing fee. The court sets a hearing and notifies the petitioner and prosecuting attorney.
ruleClauses: The source requires at least five years after completion of sentence, including probation, parole, fines, and restitution.
Only nonviolent, non-assaultive offenses covered by Idaho Code § 67-3004(11)(a)-(c) may qualify.
Only one offense, or offenses from a single incident or transaction, may be shielded, and only one Clean Slate petition may be granted in a lifetime.
The petition is filed in the county court where the underlying case was filed, with no filing fee.
The court sets a hearing and notifies the petitioner and prosecuting attorney.
Shielding prevents public access but does not destroy the records.

2. Clean Slate shielding One non-violent misdemeanor, or Court process; shield can be one felony drug-possession revoked on later conviction. I.C. conviction, after a 5-year wait §67-3004(11) from completion of sentence. One crime (or single-incident crimes) per lifetime.

pathways.label: Withheld-judgment / Idaho Code § 19-2604 review branch
pathways.summary: The Idaho State Police expungement application expressly says the non-conviction expungement process does not apply when a conviction was followed by a withheld judgment under § 19-2604(1). The frontend must route this answer to state-specific review or a separate remedy profile, not the § 67-3004(10) application. A person arrested or served with a criminal summons who was not charged by indictment or information within one year may submit a written request to Idaho State Police. A person acquitted of all offenses arising from the incident, or whose all charges were dismissed, may request expungement and court-file sealing. This route does…
ruleClauses: The Idaho State Police expungement application expressly says the non-conviction expungement process does not apply when a conviction was followed by a withheld judgment under § 19-2604(1).
The frontend must route this answer to state-specific review or a separate remedy profile, not the § 67-3004(10) application.
A person arrested or served with a criminal summons who was not charged by indictment or information within one year may submit a written request to Idaho State Police.
A person acquitted of all offenses arising from the incident, or whose all charges were dismissed, may request expungement and court-file sealing.
This route does not apply to a dismissal granted under Idaho Code § 19-2604(1), including the withheld-judgment situation described by the source.
The application requires charging documentation plus a certified acquittal or dismissal order; incomplete applications are returned.
The source says a person convicted of a sex offense should not submit this form.

Arrested or served a criminal summons, and not charged by indictment or information within one (1) year; or • Acquitted of all offenses arising from the arrest or summons; or • Had all charges dismissed. Key exclusion: this does not apply to a dismissal granted under §19-2604(1) — i.e., where a conviction was entered and then a withheld judgment was later granted. A sex-offense record is also outside this process. Successful probationers seeking conviction relief should instead look at a §19-2604 set-aside, which dismisses the charge and restores civil rights but is not an expungement.

pathways.label: Juvenile expungement
pathways.summary: 3. Juvenile expungement Most juvenile adjudications, Petition to juvenile court; records diversions, and informal sealed. I.C. §20-525A adjustments. Serious offenses excluded (murder, voluntary manslaughter, rape, arson, aggravated battery, drug trafficking, injury to a child). 4. Trafficking-survivor relief Survivors of human trafficking, Court “shall” vacate and expunge for prostitution-related and other on a preponderance finding. Serious offenses excluded (murder, voluntary manslaughter, rape, arson, aggravated battery, drug trafficking, injury to a child). 4. Idaho — Record-Clearing Reference Pack Idaho has no general adult conviction expungement. Relief runs through four narrow, track-specific routes: ISP non-conviction expungement, Clean Slate shielding, juvenile expungement, and trafficking-survivor vacatur.
Record from under age 18 Track 3 — juvenile expungement petition (§20-525A).
exclusionRules: The Idaho State Police expungement application expressly says the non-conviction expungement process does not apply when a conviction was followed by a withheld judgment under § 19-2604(1).

3. Juvenile expungement Most juvenile adjudications, Petition to juvenile court; records diversions, and informal sealed. I.C. §20-525A adjustments. Serious offenses excluded (murder, voluntary manslaughter, rape, arson, aggravated battery, drug trafficking, injury to a child). 4. Trafficking-survivor relief Survivors of human trafficking, Court “shall” vacate and expunge for prostitution-related and other on a preponderance finding. I.C. qualifying offenses that resulted §67-3014 from acts of trafficking.

copyGuardrails: Use Idaho-specific remedy terminology returned by the profile; do not relabel every remedy as expungement.
Use “may qualify,” “possible path,” and “the court or agency makes the final decision.”
Do not promise erasure, sealing, eligibility, timing, filing acceptance, or a court outcome.
Do not show checkout unless the backend returns a packet-ready result and paymentAllowed=true.

sourceSections.title: The case number for the records you want to have shielded.
sourceSections.text:
2. The exact criminal charges, including the statute(s) you were charged under and whether an
offense was a misdemeanor or a felony.
3. If applicable, the date that you were no longer under the supervision of probation or parole.
4. The date your sentence and all conditions were completed, including any fines and restitution

Obtain the case number from the court, or the iCourt Portal at: https://mycourts.idaho.gov/. If
you cannot locate your case information or have any questions about your court record, contact
the court where the case was filed.

What offenses may be eligible for shielding?

Only non-violent, non-assaultive offenses are included in the Clean Slate Act for shielding records
from public disclosure. You can review eligibility under the Clean Slate Act in Idaho Code section
67-3004(11)(a)-(c).

If you are unsure whether you meet the conditions for shielding your record from public
disclosure, you should consult with an attorney. Please note, you can only have one Clean Slate
Act shielding petition granted in your lifetime. You may want to seek legal advice for help
determining the timing and offense eligibility before filing.

01/01/2024

Step 2. Complete the Petition Form

Fill out the Petition to Shield Records from Public Disclosure.

▪ Type or fill out the Petition in black ink.
▪ Fill in your contact information at the top
of the Petition. Fill in the district, county, SAMPLE FORM

case number, and your name as the
Petitioner/Defendant. You can obtain this
information from the court if you do not
have it.
▪ Mark the checkbox that applies to your
situation.

Step 3. File your Petition with the Court

Sign and date your Petition and make a copy for your records. To file, take your original document
and copy and file it at the county court where the underlying criminal case was filed. Ask the court
clerk to date stamp your copy. Or file your form online by creating an iCourt account at
https://idaho.tylertech.cloud/OfsWeb . There is no filing fee to file your Petition.

Step 4. Attend the Hearing on your Petition

Once your Petition is filed, the court will set a hearing date and notify you and the prosecuting
attorney of the hearing date. You will receive a Notice of Hearing from the court. You can monitor
the status of your case on the iCourt Portal.

Talk to an attorney. These instructions are not a substitute for legal advice. The laws and court rules are complex and
following these instructions will not guarantee you a favorable result. We always recommend you talk with an attorney
before filing paperwork.

01/01/2024

Idaho State Police
Service since 1939

Bureau of Criminal Identification

Expungement Application
Applicant Name: ________________________________________________________________
(last name) (first name) (middle initial)
Applicant Date of Birth: ___________________________
Current Address: ________________________________________________________________
City: ___________________________________________ State:__________ ZIP:___________
Telephone Number: _______________________________
I was charged for the crime of: ___________________________________________________
I was arrested on: _________________________________________________ for this crime.
Idaho Court Case Number: ________________________________________________________
County of Court Case Number: ____________________________________________________
Charge(s) applicant seeks to have expunged: _________________________________________
_____________________________________________________________________________
Idaho Code §67-3004(10) states: Any person who was arrested or served a criminal summons
and who subsequently was not charged by indictment or information within one (1) year of the
arrest or summons and any person who was acquitted of all offenses arising from an arrest or
criminal summons, or who has had all charges dismissed, may have the fingerprint and
criminal history record taken in connection with the incident expunged pursuant to the
person’s written request directed to the department and may have the official court file
thereof sealed. This provision shall not apply to any dismissal granted pursuant to section
19-2604(1), Idaho Code.

PLEASE NOTE: If the charge(s) submitted for expungement, resulted in a conviction and then
a withheld judgement was granted by a court, per Idaho Code section 19-2604(1), the
expungement process DOES NOT apply to you.

Applicant’s Date of Acquittal or Dismissal: _________________________________

1

The applicant must also include copies of the following documents with the application:
1) A. Criminal citation, or
